name: tailwind-design-system
description: Build scalable design systems with Tailwind CSS, design tokens, component libraries, and responsive patterns. Use when creating component libraries, implementing design systems, or standardizing UI patterns.
capabilities: Generate Tailwind CSS configuration with semantic design tokens
Create component variants using CVA (Class Variance Authority)
Build compound components with proper React patterns
Implement responsive grid systems and layouts
Add dark mode support with CSS variables
Create accessible form components with validation
author: sickn33
securitySummary: Static analysis detected 78 potential issues, all confirmed as false positives. The 'backtick execution' patterns are Markdown code fences (```) for code samples. URLs are documentation references. localStorage usage is standard theme persistence. No executable code or security risks present.
valueStatement: Creating consistent design systems is complex and time-consuming. This skill provides proven patterns for building scalable component libraries with Tailwind CSS, design tokens, and accessibility built-in.
userTitle: Build design systems with Tailwind CSS
limitations: Requires React and TypeScript project setup
Does not include visual design or branding guidance
Focused on utility-first CSS approach only
Does not provide design tool integrations (Figma, Sketch)

useCases.title: Startup Component Library
useCases.description: Build a complete component library from scratch for a new product, including buttons, cards, forms, and dialogs with consistent styling.

useCases.title: Design System Migration
useCases.description: Migrate an existing CSS or styled-components codebase to Tailwind CSS with proper design token structure and component patterns.

useCases.targetUser: Developers building admin dashboards and SaaS applications
useCases.title: Accessible Form Implementation
useCases.description: Create accessible form components with proper validation, error states, and keyboard navigation following WCAG guidelines.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Button Component
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a reusable Button component using Tailwind CSS and CVA. Include variants for primary, secondary, outline, and ghost styles. Add size variants (sm, md, lg) and a disabled state.
promptTemplates.scenario: Starting a new component library and need foundational components.

promptTemplates.title: Design Token Configuration
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate a Tailwind CSS configuration file with semantic color tokens. Include primary, secondary, destructive, muted, accent, background, foreground, and border colors. Support both light and dark mode using CSS variables.
promptTemplates.scenario: Setting up the foundation for a design system.

promptTemplates.title: Compound Card Component
promptTemplates.prompt: Build a Card component using the compound component pattern. Create Card, CardHeader, CardTitle, CardDescription, CardContent, and CardFooter subcomponents. Use forwardRef for proper React composition.
promptTemplates.scenario: Creating flexible layout components for content display.

promptTemplates.title: Dark Mode Theme Provider
promptTemplates.prompt: Implement a complete dark mode solution with a ThemeProvider component. Support system preference detection, localStorage persistence, and manual toggle. Include a ThemeToggle button component with sun/moon icons.
promptTemplates.scenario: Adding theme switching to an existing application.

bestPractices: Use CSS variables for runtime theming and design tokens
Compose variants with CVA for type-safe component APIs
Always include focus-visible states for keyboard accessibility
antiPatterns: Using arbitrary values like h-[500px] instead of extending the theme
Hardcoding colors directly in components instead of semantic tokens
Skipping dark mode styles when building new components

faq.question: What is CVA and why should I use it?
faq.answer: CVA (Class Variance Authority) is a library for creating type-safe component variants. It allows you to define base styles and variant options that compose cleanly, with full TypeScript support.

faq.question: How do I add custom colors to my design system?
faq.answer: Extend the Tailwind theme in tailwind.config.ts using CSS variables. Define the color in both light and dark mode in your globals.css file for full theming support.

faq.question: Can I use this skill with vanilla CSS or other frameworks?
faq.answer: This skill focuses on Tailwind CSS specifically. For vanilla CSS or other frameworks like styled-components, different patterns and approaches would be needed.

faq.question: How do I handle component accessibility?
faq.answer: Always include proper ARIA attributes, focus states, and keyboard navigation. Use semantic HTML elements and ensure proper heading hierarchy. Test with screen readers.

faq.question: What is the compound component pattern?
faq.answer: Compound components work together to create a flexible UI. Examples include Select with SelectTrigger, SelectValue, SelectContent or Card with CardHeader, CardTitle, CardContent. This pattern enables clean composition.

faq.question: How do I migrate from Bootstrap or Material UI?
faq.answer: Start by setting up design tokens that match your existing design. Then migrate components one at a time, starting with foundational elements like buttons and inputs. Use the implementation playbook for patterns.
